mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes exceeds 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with rate hikes (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate above 4.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side bottlenecks and capacity constraints, causing short-term negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of months where INDPRO declines by more than 0.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during recovery phases (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while the CPI index remains above 290, markets price in prolonged restrictive policy and sticky inflation, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation and rising-rate regime produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of observations, or the interaction effect fails to outperform single-series models in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a healthy labor market that sustains wage growth and consumer demand, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with payroll growth (Rank IC < 0.02), or the signal reverses direction during periods of wage-price spirals.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts below 1.3 million units signals weakening residential investment and broader demand contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20d returns in > 65% of months where HOUST falls below 1.3 million, or the threshold signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid expansion in industrial production during periods of low unemployment indicates capacity utilization approaching maximum levels, creating short-term positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days due to earnings optimism.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the predictive relationship breaks down when capacity utilization exceeds historical peaks (Rank IC < 0.01).
